gaps: Browser-sniff was declined, so the CLI must not claim support for enabling Printify's UI personalization checkbox.
Crowd-sniff returned third-party `/admin/printify/...` proxy routes; they are not accepted into generation.
patterns: Use manifest files for product creation and deterministic proofing.
Use `--agent` and `--select` for nested Printify payloads.
Separate documented placeholder personalization fields from unsupported browser-only controls.

novel_features.name: Personalization Coverage Audit
novel_features.description: Audit a product for documented personalization placeholder fields, missing text/image setup, and unsupported browser-only gaps.
novel_features.rationale: Requires correlating Printify product print areas, placeholder images, and text metadata in one local view instead of reading raw nested payloads.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before publishing a personalized product or before handing a manifest to another agent.

novel_features.name: Placement Matrix
novel_features.description: Show variant, print-area, placeholder, uploaded image, x/y/scale/angle, and missing-placement rows for a product.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining products, variants, print areas, placeholder image IDs, and uploads that the API returns through separate structures.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent must prove artwork placement is consistent across variants.

novel_features.name: Catalog Margin Matrix
novel_features.description: Join catalog variants and shipping data to estimate per-variant cost and margin at a target retail price.
novel_features.rationale: Requires combining blueprint, provider, variant, and shipping tables that are expensive for an agent to reconcile manually.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this before creating a batch when product economics matter more than raw catalog browsing.

novel_features.name: Product Manifest Drift
novel_features.description: Compare an intended product manifest against the current Printify product payload after create or update.
novel_features.rationale: Requires deterministic field-level comparison across manifests, product variants, and print areas rather than a single API call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this after automation creates a product and before trusting the resulting listing.

novel_features.name: Personalized Batch Manifest Compiler
novel_features.description: Expand a reusable product manifest and CSV rows into per-product manifests using documented image and text placeholder fields.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a Printify-aware manifest transform that preserves placeholder image/text semantics without guessing undocumented UI settings.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs repeatable personalized product drafts before making API writes.

novel_features.name: Asset Reuse Map
novel_features.description: List uploaded images, where each is used, unused uploads, and products sharing the same artwork.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local join from upload inventory to product print-area image IDs across the account.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to clean upload libraries or verify that a product batch reuses the expected artwork.

novel_features.name: Fulfillment Risk Scan
novel_features.description: Flag open orders tied to risky product, variant, publish, or shipment states.
novel_features.rationale: Requires joining orders, line items, products, product variants, publish status, and shipments into one operational check.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs to find fulfillment problems before customer-service work starts.

narrative.headline: Create and audit Printify products with manifest-driven image uploads, personalization checks, and local proofing.
narrative.value_prop: This CLI covers Printify's official product, upload, catalog, order, shop, and webhook API surface, then adds workflow commands for product creation confidence. Agents can compile personalized manifests, upload images, validate placement, compare drift, and inspect fulfillment risk without parsing raw nested API payloads.
narrative.auth_narrative: Printify uses a personal access token as a bearer token. Set `PRINTIFY_API_TOKEN` in your environment or `.env`; do not pass the token as a command argument.

quickstart.comment: Find the shop ID to use for product and order work while keeping the response small.
Choose a blueprint before selecting a print provider or variants.
Upload product artwork before composing the product manifest.
Create a draft product from a prepared manifest.
Compare the intended manifest with the product that now exists in Printify.

troubleshoots.fix: Confirm `PRINTIFY_API_TOKEN` is set from `.env` or your shell, then rerun `printify-pp-cli health --agent`.

troubleshoots.symptom: Product creation fails with missing variants or print areas
troubleshoots.fix: Run catalog blueprint/provider commands first, then regenerate the manifest with valid `blueprint_id`, `print_provider_id`, `variants`, and `print_areas`.
Run `printify-pp-cli personalization-audit --product-file ./examples/sample-product.json --agent`; the CLI reports documented fields separately from unsupported browser-only controls.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI for Printify product automation that needs more than raw endpoint calls: image uploads, manifest-driven drafts, personalization field checks, placement proofing, and operational audits. It is especially useful for agents building or verifying POD product batches from local artwork and CSV inputs.

recipes.title: Find a shop with narrow output
recipes.explanation: Use this first so follow-up product and order commands target the right shop without dumping the full shop payload.

recipes.title: Compile personalized product manifests
recipes.explanation: Turn a reusable template and row data into deterministic product manifests before any API writes.

recipes.title: Proof artwork placement
recipes.explanation: Inspect only the placement columns an agent needs before publishing.

recipes.title: Check product drift after create
recipes.explanation: Confirm the remote product still matches the manifest the automation intended.

recipes.title: Scan fulfillment risk
recipes.explanation: Join orders, variants, product state, and shipment state into a single operational risk view.
